items.title: Your annual reminder that agents are the people leaking contract extensions and signings, therefore we are getting the most player friendly version of the deal.
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Your annual reminder that agents are the people leaking contract extensions and signings, therefore we are getting the most player friendly version of the deal. 

What’s often left out is the team friendly aspects of deals like non guaranteed money. We don’t always get the full context of a deal.

items.title: Quick thoughts on the DWade signing:
items.author: Derek Bodner
items.body_excerpt:
Quick thoughts on the DWade signing:
- Sixers really needed some defensive versatility at that spot.
- Solid floor spacer, albeit would love more volume / quicker release.
- Plays pretty much mistake-free basketball (5.1% turnover rate!), which will be a nice change of pace.

items.title: Okay just got to the bottom of this -- Keon Ellis's deal is a true mutual option for year 2. He's getting two years and $18 million unless both sides simultaneously opt out of it next June, which presumably would only happen if they had already agreed on his next contract. (1/2)
items.author: John Hollinger
items.body_excerpt: Okay just got to the bottom of this -- Keon Ellis's deal is a true mutual option for year 2. items.title: Nudge the Bulls cap space projection up to $23.7M after the Kam Jones waiver.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Nudge the Bulls cap space projection up to $23.7M after the Kam Jones waiver.

Chicago could waive-and-stretch Jones to create even more cap space, but we'll leave his $1.1M guarantee whole for now until we know for sure.

items.title: Great player fit at a reasonable contract number. Sixers still have plenty of work to do, but this is a nice start.
items.author: Paul Hudrick

items.title: Dean Wade will go into the Sixers non-taxpayer mid-level exception. They're now $16.6 million below the first apron hard cap.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Dean Wade will go into the Sixers non-taxpayer mid-level exception. They're now $16.6 million below the first apron hard cap.

They have about $6 million remaining of their MLE, but using it would push them up against the luxury tax line.

items.title: Keon Ellis has agreed to a 2-year, $18 million deal with the Brooklyn Nets, league sources confirmed to @fredkatz.bsky.social and me. The contract has a team AND player option for Year 2, so either side can choose to opt in.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
Keon Ellis has agreed to a 2-year, $18 million deal with the Brooklyn Nets, league sources confirmed to @fredkatz.bsky.social and me. The contract has a team AND player option for Year 2, so either side can choose to opt in. 
Shams first on it.
